based_on: Byte-precise mutation of fixtures/mazur.golden.jsonl (engine-emitted canonical bytes). The fixture differs from the golden in exactly two places: the fixture_status block (3 fields) and the single targeted numeric value.

what_was_NOT_mutated: updates[4].optimizer.factors[0].from ('backward.output_error_signals.o1.signal_value' — the provenance pointer Rule 8 resolves)
backward.output_error_signals.o1.signal_value (-0.138498562 — Rule 8 resolves the path and finds this UNMUTATED value, then compares against the mutated factor.value)
updates[4].optimizer.factors[1] (upstream_activation — w5's other factor is intact)
updates[5] (w6) and beyond — only w5's factor[0].value mutated; the same -0.138498562 value appears in w6's factor[0].value, NOT mutated here
mutation.rule_4_cascade_unavoidable: Rule 8 mutates a FACTOR value. Rule 4 multiplies factors to recompute gradient. Mutating any factor value perturbs the Rule-4 product by O(delta * other_factor). Specifically: new_product = -0.138497562 * 0.593269992 vs stored gradient -0.0821670406; delta ≈ 5.9e-7 (still > 1e-9 tolerance). So Rule 4 ALSO fires on w5. This is structurally unavoidable for the 'mutate factor.value, leave from intact' fixture design — Rule 4 cannot pass when its inputs are wrong. Rule 8 is the targeted invariant; Rule 4 is the structural cascade. The reconciler must distinguish them via the field_path: Rule 8 reports field_path = the 'from' string ('backward.output_error_signals.o1.signal_value'), Rule 4 reports field_path = 'updates[4].gradient'.

single_target_invariant: Reconciliation produces exactly one Rule 8 failure naming parameter w5 with field_path = 'backward.output_error_signals.o1.signal_value' (the 'from' path). One Rule 4 failure on updates[4].gradient is the structural cascade.
reconciliation_check_targeted_first: Rule 8: factor.value == value at the path indicated by factor.from
cascading_failures_observed: Rule 4 on updates[4].gradient (w5): product(mutated factor.value, upstream_activation) no longer matches stored gradient. Structural cascade — cannot be eliminated without making the fixture violate the 'single mutation' contract.
failure_priority_assertion: Per docs/reconciliation.md, reconciliation failures report before fixture-lifecycle failures.
purpose: Anti-circularity (Csmith / CompCert lineage): proves the reconciler checks Rule 8 (provenance reference consistency — factor.value resolves to the value at factor.from) by detecting a 1e-6 mismatch (1000x the 1e-9 tolerance). The Rule 4 cascade is acceptable and documented.
